claims.text: TriageCore is a lightweight, local-compute-first orchestration harness for token-efficient agent workflows.
Identity records are stored locally at .triagecore/identity/agents.json and map an agent_id to role, public_key, and capabilities.
Signatures attribute an action to a specific identity (cryptographic provenance).
Audit logs record the paths and transitions taken by agents during a workflow.
Humans may act as stewards who provide high-level validation requiring specific signing capabilities.
TriageCore should not claim to be a centralized, cloud-based IAM system.
TriageCore should not claim formal verification of agent logic.
TriageCore should not claim real-time network-layer security.
TriageCore should not claim full-scale LLM lifecycle management.
TriageCore should not claim distributed or multi-tenant state consistency.
Add a Governance & Identity Model documentation section describing the agents.json schema and the capability hierarchy.

declared_actions.text: Add a Governance & Identity Model documentation section describing the agents.json schema and the capability hierarchy.
